intro: Myrtoleucodendron pauciflorum, a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, is a member of the Rutaceae family and is commonly known as a source of bergamot mint or West Indian bay. The leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Myrtoleucodendron pauciflorum include limonoids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
